title: depdog work file (cross-unit governance)
description: Cross-unit dependency rules for a polyglot monorepo, enforced by `depdog check` when depdog.work.yaml sits at the checked root. Units are named root-relative subtrees; rules, boundaries and surfaces govern the dependency edges between them.

version.description: Work file format version (independent of depdog.yaml's version).

units.description: Named units. A unit is a root-relative directory subtree, usually one language project. Shorthand is a bare directory string; the expanded form takes {path, lang, config}.

path.description: Root-relative directory of the unit (a plain dir, not a glob). Ownership is subtree-prefix; nested unit dirs resolve most-specific-wins.

lang.description: Language adapter pin (e.g. go, ts, py). Optional; omitted means auto-detect from marker files.

config.description: Root-relative path of the unit's own depdog.yaml. Optional; defaults to <path>/depdog.yaml. A unit without a config is still scanned so its cross-unit edges are governed.

default.description: Fallback stance for a unit with no allow/deny rule: allow (open — may depend on any unit) or deny (strict whitelist). Optional; defaults to allow.

rules.description: Per-unit dependency rules, same vocabulary as component rules with units as the members. Refs are unit names or "*"; deny wins.

boundaries.description: Named mutual-exclusion groups of units: distinct members may not depend on each other. sealed: true adds a one-way wall (no unit outside the members may depend inward). Shorthand is a bare list of unit names; the expanded form takes {members, sealed}.

surfaces.description: Which sub-paths of a unit other units may reach. Globs are unit-relative. A target path matching internal is denied even when the unit edge is allowed; with a non-empty exports list, any other non-empty target path is denied too. An empty target path (the unit's public root, e.g. a bare package name) always passes.
